title: erigeron peregrinus subsp. peregrinus
intro: Erigeron peregrinus subsp. peregrinus, also known as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, is a type of perennial wildflower that is native to the western coast of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Erigeron peregrinus subsp. peregrinus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
